---
title: "Talks"
---

The NYC Open Data Lab is actively shared through conference presentations, public talks, and community events focused on open data, reproducible research, and data science education.

These talks highlight the Lab’s approach to building a connected ecosystem of teaching, tools, and public-facing research.

---

## Featured Talks

### NYC Open Data Week 2026  
**Teaching with NYC Open Data: Publishing Student Civic Research Through Reproducible Workflows**

- 🎥 [Watch the talk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FjDyCY4MAO4)  
- 📊 [View slides](https://martinezc1.github.io/nyc-open-data-week-2026/#/title-slide)

---

### posit::conf(2026)  
**Designing Reproducible Learning Ecosystems with R and Quarto**

- 🔗 [Conference sessions](https://conf.posit.co/2026/sessions/)

---

### useR! 2026  
**Teaching Reproducibility by Design: An End-to-End R Workflow Using Quarto, Open Data, and Package Development**

- 🔗 [Conference site](https://user2026.r-project.org/)
